name: Embalse de La Viñuela – circular from Periana por el Puerto Sábar
name_original: Embalse de La Viñuela – circular desde Periana por el Puerto Sábar
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Periana are set within the Axarquía region of Málaga, Spain, characterized by diverse landscapes. The area features the expansive Lake Viñuela, rolling hills covered in olive groves, and significant mountain ranges such as the Sierra de Alhama and Sierra de Enmedio. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from relatively flatter sections around the lake to challenging ascents with substantial elevation gains in the surrounding mountains. The region offers quiet roads and a network of routes suitable for different fitness levels. Best road cycling routes around Periana The most popular road cycling route is Embalse de La Viñuela – circular from Periana por el Puerto Sábar, a 25.1 miles (40.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 2 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic loop around Lake Viñuela, including a mountain pass. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Scenic Winding Road – Torre del Mar Lighthouse loop from Las Mayorales, a difficult 34.8 miles (55.9 km) path. This route provides coastal views and challenging terrain with significant elevation changes. Local road cyclists also love the Climb to Periana – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Taj Majal, a 30.2 miles (48.7 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Periana is defined by Lake Viñuela, extensive olive groves, and the surrounding mountain ranges, offering routes for various ability levels. The routes in Periana are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 3000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Periana's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Periana?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes available around Periana on komoot, offering diverse terrain from lakeside loops to challenging mountain ascents. These include 2 easy, 27 moderate, and 25 difficult routes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Periana?
A: Road cycling around Periana offers varied terrain. You'll find relatively flatter sections around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939641218/embalse-de-la-vinuela-circular-from-periana-por-el-puerto-sabar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Viñuela&lt;/a&gt;, rolling hills covered in olive groves, and significant climbs into the surrounding mountain ranges like the Sierra de Alhama and Sierra de Enmedio. Expect quiet roads and substantial elevation gains, especially when venturing inland.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the Periana area?
A: Yes, Periana is known for its challenging climbs. The region's terrain often involves significant elevation gains, with some routes reaching over 1,700 meters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40646034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Periana – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Taj Majal&lt;/a&gt; offers over 1,000 meters of climbing, providing a rewarding challenge for experienced riders.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939641218/embalse-de-la-vinuela-circular-from-periana-por-el-puerto-sabar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Viñuela&lt;/a&gt;. You'll also cycle through extensive olive groves and past the Sierra de Alhama and Sierra de Enmedio mountain ranges. Notable viewpoints include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5272042" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boquete de Zafarraya Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/381190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zalía and Monjes Hills - View of Salia&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also close to the &lt;a href="https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sierras_of_Tejeda,_Almijara_and_Alhama_Natural_Park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, offering rugged landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Periana?
A: Yes, there are several circular road cycling routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939641218/embalse-de-la-vinuela-circular-from-periana-por-el-puerto-sabar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de La Viñuela – circular from Periana por el Puerto Sábar&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic loop around the lake. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164930" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Viñuela Reservoir – Alcaucín Village loop from Venta El Charco&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Periana?
A: The road cycling routes around Periana are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 60 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, quiet roads, and the rewarding challenges offered by the climbs and varied terrain.

Q: Is Periana a good destination for family-friendly road cycling?
A: While many routes in Periana involve significant elevation, there are a few easier options suitable for families or those seeking a more relaxed ride. These routes often trace the shores of Lake Viñuela, providing flatter sections. It's advisable to check the difficulty and elevation profile of specific routes before heading out with children.

Q: What is the best time of year to go road cycling in Periana?
A: The region around Periana generally offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures, ideal for tackling the climbs. Winter can also be suitable, though higher elevations might be cooler. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended during peak season.

Q: Are there any specific amenities like cafes or accommodation for cyclists in Periana?
A: Periana and the surrounding villages offer various amenities. You'll find cafes and restaurants where you can refuel, and there are accommodation options catering to visitors. While not specifically listed as 'cyclist-friendly' in all cases, the area is accustomed to outdoor enthusiasts.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for road cycling in the Periana area?
A: There is no indication that specific permits are required for road cycling on the general routes around Periana. However, if you plan to venture into protected areas like the Sierras de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park, it's always wise to check for any specific regulations, especially if combining cycling with other activities like hiking.

Q: Can I find bike rental services in Periana?
A: While specific bike rental services in Periana are not detailed in the provided information, it is a popular cycling destination. It's recommended to check local tourism offices or search online for bike hire shops in Periana or nearby larger towns in the Axarquía region before your visit.

Q: What are some of the more difficult road cycling routes for advanced riders?
A: For advanced riders seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558581" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Winding Road – Torre del Mar Lighthouse loop from Las Mayorales&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and coastal views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40646034" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Climb to Periana – Puerto del Sol (1085 m) loop from Taj Majal&lt;/a&gt; is another demanding option, featuring over 1,000 meters of ascent.
tips: Enjoy the views of the viewpoint
